question: Will UNP's market close price on 2026-06-28 be higher than its market close price on 2026-06-21? Stock splits and reverse splits will be accounted for in resolving this question. Forecasts on questions about companies that have been delisted (through mergers or bankruptcy) will resolve to their final close price. Resolution criteria: Resolves to the market close price at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/UNP for the resolution date. If the resolution date coincides with a day the market is closed (weekend, holiday, etc.) the previous market close price is used. Background: Union Pacific Corporation, through its subsidiary, Union Pacific Railroad Company, operates in the railroad business in the United States. It offers transportation services for grain and grain products, fertilizers, food and refrigerated products, and coal and renewables to grain processors, animal feeders, and ethanol and renewable biofuel producers; and construction products, industrial chemicals, plastics, forest products, specialized products, metals and ores, petroleum, liquid petroleum gases, soda ash, and sand, as well as finished automobiles, automotive parts, and merchandise in intermodal containers. The company was founded in 1862 and is headquartered in Omaha, Nebraska.

Background: Union Pacific Corporation, through its subsidiary, Union Pacific Railroad Company, operates in the railroad business in the United States. It offers transportation services for grain and grain products, fertilizers, food and refrigerated products, and coal and renewables to grain processors, animal feeders, and ethanol and renewable biofuel producers; and construction products, industrial chemicals, plastics, forest products, specialized products, metals and ores, petroleum, liquid petroleum gases, soda ash, and sand, as well as finished automobiles, automotive parts, and merchandise in intermodal containers. The company was founded in 1862 and is headquartered in Omaha, Nebraska.

evidences.title: Union Pacific Corp Stock (UNP) Closed Up by 3.21% on Jun 5: Key Drivers Unveiled
evidences.content: Further supporting the upward trend is an improving outlook for the freight cycle. Reports indicate broadening freight growth, with gains across most major commodity groups. This suggests increased economic momentum and resilient goods demand, which directly benefits rail stocks like UNP. Specifically, international container volume on rail has seen an 8% year-over-year increase, while domestic intermodal traffic is up 14%, demonstrating a sustained replenishment activity and a willingness among shippers to utilize rail. [...] Analyst sentiment remains largely positive, with Union Pacific holding a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from numerous Wall Street analysts. The median price target from recent analyst reports suggests further upside potential. This positive analytical perspective, along with the company's solid financial performance, including better-than-expected earnings and revenue in its first quarter of 2026, contributes to investor confidence. The company reported strong operating revenue and increased earnings per share for the first quarter, affirming its 2026 outlook for mid-single-digit EPS growth and consistent annual dividend increases. evidences.title: Union Pacific Corporation (UNP) Analyst: Paul Richter- ...
evidences.content: Union Pacific Corporation (UNP) Analyst: Paul Richter-Trummer pg. 1 Recommendation: SELL Estimated Fair Value: $80 – $110 1. Reasons for the Recommendation The growth in revenues for the railroad industry is expected to slow in the years to come. Since 2006, excluding the drop in revenues in 2009, the industry revenues have been growing at a rate of 5.5% per year. Union Pacific was able to grow faster than its industry in these years, with growth of 9.8% in revenues per year. The average growth in industry revenue per year over the next 6 years is projected to be around 3% per year.1 The consensus amongst analyst is a forecast with an annual growth in earnings of around 16% over the next 5 years for Union Pacific, leading to the high median target price of $128.2 It seems unlikely that [...] Union Pacific is currently trading at a higher multiple (Enterprise Value over Revenue) than its competitors in the railroad industry. This can be explained with Union Pacific’s lower dependency on the coal business. But even when adjusting the number by using a weighted multiple for each business segment, the multiple analysis still points to Union Pacific being overvalued compared to its c...

evidences.title: Union Pacific (UNP) Stock Forecast: Key Catalysts Driving 2026 Momentum
evidences.content: ## Key Takeaways Upcoming Q1 2026 earnings on April 23 expected to deliver EPS of around $2.85, highlighting operational resilience amid volume challenges. Analyst consensus leans "Moderate Buy" with an average price target of approximately $270, implying potential upside from current levels. Strategic network investments position UNP for efficiency gains and service improvements in a recovering freight market. Rail industry tailwinds include service enhancements and potential merger activity, though volume growth hinges on industrial production rebound. Key macro sensitivities to GDP growth, trade policies, and interest rates could boost intermodal and industrial volumes. Risks include persistent soft freight demand and inflationary pressures on operating costs. [...] The near-term spotlight falls on UNP's Q1 2026 earnings release on April 23, where analysts anticipate EPS of $2.85 and revenue near $6.1 billion, reflecting pricing discipline despite softer volumes. Strong execution could prompt positive revisions to full-year guidance, building on recent analyst upgrades like Benchmark's price target hike to $275 (Buy) and Evercore ISI's shift to Outperform at $262. evidences.content: Buy 41% Hold 29% Sell 0% Strong Sell 0% #### Bulls say Union Pacific's projected annual free cash flow (FCF) is expected to reach approximately $12 billion by Year 3, which will enhance its financial capability for ongoing network investments and bolster dividend commitments. The company's extensive network of over 30,000 miles of track positions it as a crucial player in the U.S. freight market, facilitating trade not only domestically but also through its partnership with Mexican railroad Ferromex. Furthermore, the expanded network is anticipated to improve international connections, increasing competitiveness with Canadian ports and enhancing overall access to global trade markets. #### Bears say [...] #### Bears say Union Pacific faces significant risks that contribute to a negative outlook, including uncertainties related to growth from key shipping ports like Prince Rupert and Halifax, as well as severe weather conditions that may disrupt crop quality and network efficiency. evidences.title: June 2026 Monthly Rail Recap - Trains
evidences.content: Spot Rate Surge: Truckload spot rates have surged to $3.55/mile, while truckload contract rates sit at $2.58/mile—up approximately 10% year-over-year. Intermodal Rate Stability: Intermodal spot rates remain at $1.64/mile, with contract rates holding steady at $1.50/mile. Rejection Rates Climbing: The SONAR Truckload Tender Rejection Index has jumped to 15.4%, compared to just 7% a year ago. This spike was driven by International Roadcheck week and the Memorial Day holiday, when many carriers came off the road. Spot Above Contract: Truckload spot rates have now crossed above contract rates—a clear signal that contract renewals will see significant increases as agreements roll over. ### 3. Intermodal Savings: The Value Proposition Widens [...] Year-Over-Year Growth: Domestic intermodal volume is up approximately 13% year-over-year, an unusually strong showing for this time of year. Record-Breaking Quarter: March marked a record month for domestic volume, and the momentum carried through April and May with sustained strength. Pre-Holiday Surge: Even the pre-Memorial Day peak was higher than last year, underscoring robust demand for containerized rail freight. Key Drivers: Strong se...

evidences.title: Union Pacific Corporate History
evidences.content: April 25, 1995 Union Pacific Corporation completed the purchase of 71.6 percent of Chicago & Northwestern Transportation Company stock, giving it a total of 99.5 percent of all CNWT stock. (SEC, Union Pacific Form 10-Q, dated August 14, 1995) May 1, 1995 UP purchase and control of C&NW became effective. (SEC, Union Pacific Form 10-Q, dated August 14, 1995) July 25, 1995 UP's board of directors approved the merger deal with SP. August 3, 1995 UP and SP announced that the two railroads had signed an agreement to merge. The offer was reported as $5.4 billion. (BusinessWeek, August 20, 1995) [...] In 1995, Union Pacific Corporation organized all of its natural resource operations (Champlin Petroleum and Rocky Mountain Energy) into the Union Pacific Resources Group, Inc. In October 1995, Union Pacific sold a 17 percent stake to the public in an IPO. (New York Times, October 9, 1995) On October 15, 1996, Union Pacific Corporation spun off its remaining 83 percent stake in Union Pacific Resources Group. At the time, Union Pacific Resources owned 7.5 million acres of land in the western states that originally came to Union Pacific as mid-19th century land grants. (New York Times, Novemb...

evidences.title: Rail Insider-Outlook 2026: Class I leaders keep the faith that business will be slightly better next year. Information For Rail Career Professionals From Progressive Railroading Magazine
evidences.content: We have launched an intense boots-on-the-ground sales program that is chasing every opportunity, no matter the size. This effort has brought in $35 million in the third quarter and is closing in on $100 million in the fourth quarter, and it’s helping offset weakness in other areas. We know where our capacity is and will be aggressive in selling into it. VENA: As Union Pacific looks ahead at 2026, there are always macro issues that can affect us. If the U.S. and global economies continue to move ahead, we see opportunities across several areas of our diverse franchise to continue growing our business, including in intermodal and manifest. [...] The team also is doubling down on productivity efforts. Adjusting cost structures is critical, especially in a soft macro environment, and we’re pursuing all opportunities across our full workforce and asset base, including taking $75 million out of management labor costs as part of our plan to continue to drive improvement in our operating efficiency. We know there’s more to get here, and we’re after it. VENA: Our “Safety, Service and Operational Excellence” strategy continues to be our foundation. Railroading will always be full of ups a...

evidences.title: Union Pacific (NYSE:UNP): Rail Stock Watched as Transport Investors Track Freight Demand
evidences.content: Valuation and Market-Cap Context The market-capitalisation figure in the source data is about US$167.58 billion, reflecting the company's standing among US stocks. On the source data, the stock trades on a P/E ratio of roughly 23.52. A multiple in this region is fairly typical for an established company, though valuation always needs to be judged against growth prospects and sector norms. It also helps to view valuation in context. For a rail and freight transport company, investors typically compare the shares against sector peers and against the company's own history, rather than in isolation. A figure that looks high or low means little without that comparison, and market participants are assessing the full mosaic. [...] Sector Background The rail sector is a barometer of the goods economy, hauling commodities, industrial products and intermodal containers. Operators focus on efficiency, pricing and network fluidity, and results can be affected by weather, fuel costs and supply-chain shifts. Market participants are assessing how freight demand and efficiency initiatives shape earnings. Within this landscape, Union Pacific represents one way investors gain exposure to the rail...

evidences.title: AAR reports rail traffic for the week ending June 06, 2026 | AJOT.COM
evidences.content: For the first 22 weeks of 2026, U.S. railroads reported cumulative volume of 4,984,985 carloads, up 3.3 percent from the same point last year; and 6,113,730 intermodal units, up 2.3 percent from last year. Total combined U.S. traffic for the first 22 weeks of 2026 was 11,098,715 carloads and intermodal units, an increase of 2.7 percent compared to last year. [...] North American rail volume for the week ending June 6, 2026, on 9 reporting U.S., Canadian and Mexican railroads totaled 333,030 carloads, up 1.7 percent compared with the same week last year, and 380,156 intermodal units, up 11.0 percent compared with last year. Total combined weekly rail traffic in North America was 713,186 carloads and intermodal units, up 6.4 percent. North American rail volume for the first 22 weeks of 2026 was 15,281,170 carloads and intermodal units, up 2.4 percent compared with 2025. evidences.content: Union Pacific currently trades on a P/E of 22.45x. This sits below the Transportation industry average P/E of about 39.94x and below the peer group average of 30.64x. Simply Wall St's Fair Ratio for Union Pacific is 28.63x, which is a proprietary estimate of what the P/E could reasonably be given factors such as earnings growth, profit margins, industry, market cap and risk profile. Because it blends these company specific drivers, the Fair Ratio can be more tailored than a simple comparison with peers or a broad industry average. With Union Pacific's actual P/E at 22.45x versus a Fair Ratio of 28.63x, the stock appears undervalued on this metric. Result: UNDERVALUED NYSE:UNP P/E Ratio as at Jun 2026 [...] Using this 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, the DCF model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of $325.95 per share, compared with the recent share price of $272.70. That gap implies the stock trades at a 16.3% discount to the DCF estimate, which points to a meaningful margin between price and modeled value. evidences.title: Union Pacific Stock Outlook: Is Wall Street Bullish or Bearish?
evidences.content: Shares of this leading class 1 railroad company have underperformed the broader market over the past year. UNP has gained 16% over this time frame, while the broader S&P 500 Index ($SPX) has rallied nearly 26.9%. However, in 2026, UNP stock is up 14.4%, surpassing SPX's 9.5% rise on a YTD basis. ### More News from Barchart Dear Broadcom Stock Fans, Mark Your Calendars for June 3 Buying CBRS Stock After the Cerebras IPO Is a Bet on Engineering Magic. That Same Magic Could Be the Kiss of Death. Cathie Wood Remains Bullish About Tesla's Robotaxi. TSLA Stock Is Waiting for a Financial Trigger Higher. Markets move fast. Keep up by reading our FREE midday Barchart Brief newsletter for exclusive charts, analysis, and headlines. [...] ... Barchart # Union Pacific Stock Outlook: Is Wall Street Bullish or Bearish? evidences.title: Union Pacific merger family tree | Trains Magazine
evidences.content: ## Denver & Salt Lake Railway Construction began from Denver west in 1902 as the Denver, Northwestern & Pacific. It was then organized in July 1912 as the Denver & Salt Lake Railway. Moffat Tunnel, named after railroad founder David H. Moffat, opened on Feb. 26, 1928. The Denver & Rio Grande Western acquired D&SL stock, beginning in the 1930s, and merged on April 11, 1947. ## Western Pacific Railroad Taking the same name as the original Sacramento-Oakland part of the Central Pacific, Western Pacific was incorporated in 1903 to extend the Gould empire west of Utah. It was finished in 1909. Both the Santa Fe and Southern Pacific wanted the WP in 1962, but neither got it. Union Pacific ultimately did, merging on Dec. 22, 1981. ## Sacramento Northern Railway [...] ## St. Louis Southwestern Railway St. Louis Southwestern began as the 3-foot-gauge Tyler Tap Railroad, opened in 1877 in eastern Texas. Its successor, St. Louis, Arkansas & Texas, was standard-gauged in 1886. The nickname “Cotton Belt Route” is historic with the emblem, designed by Charles Ware at the railroad’s request, replaced by a cotton bale in 1885. StLA&T was acquired by the new St. Louis Southwestern of Jay Gould i...

News NYSE:UNP 1D 1W 3M 1Y 5Y All $ Market data provided byMassive ### All News View all Research Report #### Union Pacific (UNP) Research Report: Q1 CY2026 Update Freight transportation company Union Pacific (NYSE:UNP) met Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 3.2% year on year to $6.22 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $2.93 per share was 2.4% above analysts’ consensus estimates. 2 months ago Analysis [...] ## 4. Rail Transportation The growth of e-commerce and global trade continues to drive demand for shipping services, presenting opportunities for rail transportation companies. While moving large volumes by rail can be highly cost-efficient for customers compared to air and ground transport, this mode of transportation results in slower delivery times, presenting a trade off. evidences.title: [PDF] UNION PACIFIC RAILROAD
evidences.content: We have an opportunity to grow carload volumes of renewable energy, including biofuels. We provide value to renewable fuels producers by transporting low-carbon fuels to the West Coast, where demand is the highest and producers can benefit from state and federal incentives. Our trains are ideal for shipping large volumes of freight, including renewable fuels and feedstocks. Compared to shipping by truck, we can reduce our customers’ logistics carbon footprint and simultaneously provide cost savings on their shipments by delivering feedstocks to biofuels refineries and then transporting renewable fuels to consumption markets. evidences.title: Union Pacific History
evidences.content: The Union Pacific-Southern Pacific merger takes effect, forming the largest railroad in the U.S. The new system covers 31,000 miles through 24 states and runs 2,000 trains a day. The 2002 Salt Lake Olympic Torch Relay Cauldron Car, provided by Union Pacific, travels through the desert between Albuquerque, New Mexico, and Tuscon, Arizona, during the 2002 Salt Lake City Olympic Torch Relay. Union Pacific operates a special train – featuring two specially painted locomotives and a cauldron car – carrying the Olympic flame across 11 states over more than 3,200 miles enroute to the Winter Games in Salt Lake City, Utah. Photo Credit: Todd Warshaw/Pool/Getty Images Close-up of UP 4014 a day prior to the Great Race to Promontory event in Odgen, Utah. [...] Otto Jabelmann, vice president of research and mechanical standards, standing on front steps of the new Big Boy locomotive no. 4000. Part of a series of photographs made of Big Boy locomotive no. 4000 in September 1941. The largest steam locomotive ever built, the Big Boy No. 4014, goes into service on Union Pacific lines. This same year, diesel power is introduced for freight service. Spokane, Washington Union Pacific acquires Spokan...

evidences.title: Governance Overview | Union Pacific Railroad Company
evidences.content: ## Corporate Governance Overview The Board of Directors, the Corporate Governance, Nominating and Sustainability Committee and the management of Union Pacific are united in the goal that the Company and its subsidiaries be among the best governed companies in America. The Board first adopted formal corporate governance guidelines and policies in 1996. Each year, we have included the guidelines and policies in our Proxy Statement for the Annual Meeting of Shareholders to apprise investors and other interested parties of the Board's point of view with respect to significant corporate governance issues.
